title: What an American thinks about Italy

transcript.text: Italy Italy I loved Italy Italy is like one of those countries that you get that spiritual vibe
Off of like you know like England is like that Peru's like that big time um Nepal um Australia
Even Korea I got that off of maybe even Japan but yeah like it was a cool place dude and they
The people there seemed nice unlike France or Spain the people there seemed like they had zero
Problem with me being an American other than you know me I did I was like some street kids tried
To like pickpocket me one day they like all swarmed around me like all these like teenager street kids
Like surrounded me and were held up this like map right to my face and we're like where are we where
Are we and I was like I was like trying to be friendly like oh you know I was gonna like show
Them they totally could have robbed me if they wanted to and then all of a sudden I feel like
This like like weight come down on my backpack like as if my flap on the top of my backpack came down
And then they're like okay what never mind and they ran off like all of a sudden they like suddenly
Knew like where they were and and uh they'd never stole anything from me so maybe maybe like a
Passerby stopped and started staring at them and freaked them out and they ran off but
Um I actually had an Italian friend who told stories about how you used to do that kind of stuff and
So I guess there's lots of like hoodlumism there but but as far as like the people they were like cool

But a weird thing about Spain is they uh you have to pay extra just to eat inside of a place so if
